pattern: Simultaneous two-robot top-to-bottom DP where you track both column positions at each row and avoid double-counting cells where both robots overlap.
hints: Process the grid row by row; at each row you need to know both robots' column positions — that's a 3D DP state: (row, col1, col2).
Because robots start at opposite ends you can enforce col1 <= col2 to cut the state space in half; when col1 == col2 only one robot collects.
For each transition, each robot can move to one of three columns in the next row, giving 9 combinations to check per state.

edge_inputs.description: 2x2 grid — minimum size, robots start adjacent
Two-column grid where robots must overlap every row
Robots must share a column midway — only one cherry collected there
Rich diagonal concentration — greedy path fails

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = size
    cols = size
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 100) for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
    return [grid]
